hypothesis: If transfer learning (pre-training on large chemical datasets followed by fine-tuning on a curated Covalent Triazine Framework dataset) combined with reinforcement learning using expert-defined rewards for flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric hindrance, and pattern matching is applied, then the generated CTF candidates will exhibit higher validity, greater diversity and novelty, and a larger proportion passing expert filters than configurations without such transfer learning and RL, because domain-relevant pre-training and reward-guided optimization bias generation toward CTF-relevant chemical space and structural criteria.
